title: Urban Heat Islands
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Urban heat islands are areas where cities experience significantly higher temperatures (0) than surrounding rural regions. This phenomenon occurs because buildings, roads, and other infrastructure absorb and retain heat more effectively than natural landscapes.||The effect can be dramatic, (9) with city centres sometimes being up to 7 degrees Celsius warmer than nearby countryside. Several factors contribute (10) to this temperature difference. Dark surfaces like asphalt and concrete absorb solar radiation rather (11) than reflecting it. Additionally, cities have less vegetation, which means (12) there is less cooling through evapotranspiration – the process by which plants release water vapour.||Urban heat islands have serious consequences (13) for city residents. Higher temperatures increase energy consumption as people use more air conditioning, leading to higher electricity bills and increased greenhouse gas emissions. Heat waves can (14) be particularly dangerous for elderly people and those with health conditions.||Cities are implementing various strategies to combat urban heat islands. Planting more trees and creating green spaces (15) helps reduce temperatures naturally. Some cities are painting roofs white or installing 'cool roofs' that reflect sunlight. Green roofs covered with vegetation are (16) also becoming popular, providing insulation and reducing the urban heat island effect while creating habitats for wildlife.

example.text: Urban heat islands are areas where cities experience significantly higher temperatures (0) surrounding rural regions.

questions.explanation: Preposition 'with' introduces information about the magnitude of the effect.
Verb 'contribute to' means to contribute to something.
Comparison 'rather than' contrasts absorbing with reflecting.
Estructura 'there is' para indicar existencia de menos enfriamiento.
Expression 'consequences for' indicates who bears the consequences.
Simple present: 'helps reduce' indicates how strategies help.
Adverb 'also' adds another popular solution to the problem.
